parsed_review.summary: CP00-586 is a descriptor-only RP19 (Client Portal) coverage pack over 10 planned units (RP19.P02.M06.S03-S12), continuing the M06 cycle. Independent read-only verification (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claims are supported and all boundaries are intact. (1) Distribution: I tabulated all 10 plan units (closeout-pack-plan.json:405) and they exactly match the baseline deliverable_distribution {implementation:6,security_audit:2,ui:2} and micro_title_distribution {Synthetic Fixture Set:10}, and match registry.js CP586_REQUIREMENTS, the manifest plan_binding_snapshot (unit_count 10, deviation_from_plan false), and the contract; range/first/last unit IDs are consistent everywhere. (2) Contract: program.current_pack_id and current_pack are CP00-586 (contract:10,16-31); the p02_fixture_slice_descriptor projection (contract:5225), case-set section_count 1 with 10 rows (contract:5244-5264), and historical_pack_bindings tail (contract:5584) are all consistent, and the validator/test deepEqual-lock every projection (descriptor, requirements, no_write_attestation) — see validate-rp19-client-portal-core-contract.mjs:82-83,235-241 and model.test.js:250-298. (3) Source is pure Object.freeze descriptor generation (service.js freezeCp586Result, registry.js bindings/requirements, validators.js coverage+descriptor validators) with extensive no-write/no-real-data attestations (writes_product_state:false, no_real_data:true, evaluates_runtime_permission:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, exposes_*_detail:false, acquires_runtime_lock:false, persists_idempotency_key:false); no filesystem writes, DB, or network in any in-scope file; all 7 required public exports are present via index.js. (4) production_ready is NOT over-claimed: command-evidence and manifest both report production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, implementation_layer:descriptor, status implemented_pending_review; the Hermes production_ready_candidate flag is a candidate gated behind human final approval and downstream runtime opening (CP00-587). The standing production_ready gate is not weakened. (5) Claude stays a read-only reviewer and not final approver (review-request tools Read/Grep/Glob, read_only:true; manifest claude_not_final_approver:true; descriptor authority_boundary claude_is_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true); Hermes (H19) is used only for deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors, not product runtime (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false, no_hermes_runtime_receipt:true). I verified the prompt/baseline authority_boundary token enterprise_trust_claim_closed:true is the established review-input framing (identical in CP584/CP585 prompts) meaning the claim is closed outside this receipt; prior reviews correctly emit enterprise_trust_claim_closed:false, and the review-result schema here carries no such field — so this is not a gate weakening. (6) CP178+ evidence hardening is sound: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1444-byte valid non-empty JSON, no stderr); forbidden_review_evidence/invalid_review_blockers are present in registry; valid_review_receipts:[] and invalid_review_attempts:[] (this is the first valid review, no smuggled raw-output/receipt artifacts). (7) Changed-file scope is respected: 8 active client-portal implementation refs plus the pack docs dir; unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room data/html, workbook docs, the v2.0 docx, the LDIP invalid-attempt, new-session-handoff.md) were not inferred into the pack (active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted:[]). The expected post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are absent, which is correct at this pre-review/pending stage per the hardening sequence and is not a defect. I also independently confirmed the CP586 descriptor validator's reliance on base per-row guards plus descriptor-result-level leak locks is sufficient: none of the CP586 row keys carry ROW_EXTRAS, and freezeCp586Result locks exposes_*:false/customer_safe_errors_only:true into the contract via deepEqual, so the CP585-P3-02 defense-in-depth note does not yield an active gap here. Only one non-blocking P3 observation is raised. Note: human final approval remains required for the enterprise trust claim; Claude is not the final approver.

findings.title: M06 micro-title 'Synthetic Fixture Set' does not match its service-cycle row titles
findings.details: The RP19.P02.M06 section carries micro_title 'Synthetic Fixture Set' (registry.js:567,578; contract:5251) but its ten CP586 rows are service-cycle titles — 'Tenant boundary precheck', 'Matter trace precheck', 'Permission precheck', 'Audit hint precheck', 'Primary happy path', 'Secondary workflow path', 'State transition enforcement', 'Idempotency key handling', 'Lock acquisition rule', 'Persistence boundary' (registry.js:575) — with deliverable types implementation/security_audit/ui rather than 'fixture'. These read as Scope-Inventory/Contract-cycle rows, not fixture rows. The mismatch is faithfully and consistently reflected across the plan ledger (closeout-pack-plan.json:425-525), manifest, contract (contracts/client-portal-core-contract.json:5249-5264), registry, and README, so it is internally consistent. The mismatch originates in the upstream weighted-implementation-ledger micro-cycle generator (source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65...), not in this pack, and recurs from CP585-P3-01 (CP585 covered M06.S01-S02 with the same label). No functional or boundary impact.
findings.recommendation: No change required for CP00-586 closeout. If the plan generator's micro-cycle labeling is revisited upstream, align RP19.P02.M06 row titles with the 'Synthetic Fixture Set' micro title (or relabel the micro) for reader clarity across the M06 slice.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged; non-blocking. Faithful, internally consistent projection of the upstream weighted-implementation-ledger; out of this pack's authority to change. Same root cause as CP585-P3-01.
